channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Canada's Liberals will elect new leader to replace Trudeau as country deals with Trump's trade war
item.description: Canada’s governing Liberals are set to choose a replacement for Prime Minister Justin Trudeau. The announcement will come as the country deals with U.S. President Donald Trump’s tariff threats and as a federal election looms.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Air Force intercepts aircraft flying in a restricted zone near Mar-a-Lago
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Air Force fighter jets intercepted a civilian aircraft flying in the temporarily restricted airspace near Donald Trump's Florida home Sunday, bringing the number of violations to more than 20 since the president took office on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canadians feel ‘under economic attack,’ frustration with US over Trump tariffs, annexation talk: ambassador
item.description: Canadian Ambassador to the U.S. Kirsten Hillman said citizens "don't really appreciate" President Donald Trump's talk about annexing Canada and feel "frustrated" over his tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden's 'autopen signature' appears on most official docs, raising concerns over who controlled the WH: report
item.description: President Biden's signature on official executive documents shows the same autopen signature, renewing concerns over his mental acuity, a report shows.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump downplays business concerns about uncertainty from his tariffs and prospect of higher prices
item.description: President Donald Trump is dismissing business concerns over the uncertainty caused by his planned tariffs on a range of American trading partners and the prospect of higher prices.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Top US health agency makes $25,000 buyout offer to most of its employees
item.description: Most of the 80,000 federal workers responsible for researching diseases, inspecting food and administering Medicare and Medicaid under the auspices of the Health and Human Services Department were emailed an offer to leave their job for as much as a $25,000 payment.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Seitz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Musk and DOGE try to slash government by cutting out those who answer to voters
item.description: Conservatives have long talked about cutting government, but Republicans in Congress have shied away from approving deep reductions because of fear of how voters would react.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Riccardi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump's transactional approach to diplomacy is a driving force on the world stage
item.description: Donald Trump's style of diplomacy is a whole lot like a high-stakes business deal, and his No. 1 goal is to come out of the transaction on top.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Vance ramps up his clout in Trump world
item.description: Vice President Vance’s clout is on the rise, taking on the role of pot stirrer at the White House and on the world stage.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota lawmakers consider allowing heavier ATVs
item.description: The ATV industry says consumer preferences are shifting toward larger side-by-side vehicles. Some environmental groups question whether heavier vehicles could cause more trail damage.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Meet Sandra Whitehouse, whose husband's votes funneled millions into NGO that pays her
item.description: Sen. Sheldon Whitehouse’s wife, Sandra Whitehouse, has long worked with, and been compensated by, a nonprofit that got millions in federal funds from legislation that he backed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Travel headaches show need for feds to focus on airline tech jobs as true major delay culprit revealed
item.description: Airline safety is back in the news after several tragic incidents in US skies, and airline maintenance jobs are now at a premium, experts and lawmakers said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House Republicans unveil bill to avoid shutdown and they're daring Democrats to oppose it
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — House Republicans unveiled a spending bill Saturday that would keep federal agencies funded through Sept. 30, pushing ahead with a go-it-alone strategy that seems certain to spark a major confrontation with Democrats over the contours of government spending.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: JD Vance says he was confronted by pro-Ukrainian protesters while walking with toddler daughter
item.description: Vice President JD Vance said Saturday he was accosted by pro-Ukrainian protesters who scared his 3-year-old daughter a week after his tense exchange with the Ukrainian president.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blue state mayor proposes city ordinance to strengthen transgender care protections in the face of Trump EOs
item.description: Democratic Seattle Mayor Bruce Harrell proposed city legislation this week to strengthen protections for people seeking transgender care and surgeries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tribes and Native American students sue over Bureau of Indian Education firings
item.description: Three tribal nations and five Native American students say in a lawsuit that the Trump administration has failed its legal obligations to tribes when it cut jobs at Bureau of Indian Education schools.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Unions ask court to stop DOGE from accessing Social Security data of millions of Americans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A group of labor unions are asking a federal court for an emergency order to stop Elon Musk 's Department of Government Efficiency from accessing the sensitive Social Security data of millions of Americans.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House unleashes on 'rogue bureaucrats' after agency head refuses DOGE entry to headquarters
item.description: The president of the U.S. African Development Foundation filed a lawsuit against the Department of Government Efficiency after the cost-cutting team entered the agency's office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump order denies student loan relief to nonprofit workers engaged in 'improper' activity
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is ordering changes to the Public Service Loan Forgiveness program that would disqualify workers of nonprofit groups deemed to have engaged in “improper” activities.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump admin investigation finds Maine Dept. of Education violated Title IX
item.description: On Feb. 25, the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services sent a notice of violation against the Maine Department of Education.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota among states that sued Trump administration over federal workers' mass firings
item.description: The lawsuit says the mass firings will cause irreparable burdens and expenses on the states, because they will have to support recently unemployed workers and adjudicate claims of unemployment assistance.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: War heroes and military firsts are among 26,000 images flagged for removal in Pentagon's DEI purge
item.description: References to a World War II Medal of Honor recipient, the Enola Gay aircraft that dropped an atomic bomb on Japan and women and minorities are among the tens of thousands of photos and online posts marked for deletion as the Defense Department works to purge diversity, equity and inclusion content.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Hispanic Caucus demands apology from GOP campaign arm after ‘illegal immigrant’ smear
item.description: The Congressional Hispanic Caucus (CHC) is demanding an apology from the House Republicans’ campaign arm after it falsely portrayed the CHC’s chair as an “illegal immigrant.” The National Congressional Campaign Committee (NRCC) deleted the controversial social media post Friday.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Watch live: Fed chair Powell gives remarks on state of economy
item.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell will give an update on the state of the economy Friday afternoon — less than two months since President Trump returned to the Oval Office. The remarks, part of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Homeland Security ends collective bargaining agreement with TSA staffers, an attack on worker rights
item.description: A union representing Transportation Security Administration workers calls the Department of Homeland Security's decision to end the collective bargaining agreement with them an “unprovoked attack.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: New fines and awareness help reduce litter on Minnesota lakes, but advocates want more action
item.description: Lake advocates say a recent state law is helping curb the problem of trash left on Minnesota’s frozen lakes. But they're hoping for more action, including passage of a bill requiring state agencies establish a statewide “Keep It Clean” program and provide funding for local cleanup efforts.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Trump limits Musk's power as federal workforce concerns grow
item.description: While new changes to the federal workforce may be limited, Musk's orders have already reshaped several federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Hena Doba, NewsNation

item.title: Trump is set to speak to crypto founders and investors at the White House on Friday
item.description: President Trump signed an order late Thursday creating a cryptocurrency strategic reserve using bitcoin seized through court proceedings, directing officials to find “budget-neutral” ways to grow it.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: Former US solicitor general to submit written arguments in Eric Adams case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former U.S. solicitor general who was brought in to help a federal judge decide whether to accept a Justice Department request to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams is set to submit written arguments Friday. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump says he’s 'looking at' revoking protected status for Ukrainians
item.description: “We're not looking to hurt anybody, and we're certainly not looking to hurt them,” Trump told reporters in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trump passes Clinton, gives longest modern address to joint session of Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump gave the longest address to a joint session of Congress in modern history on Tuesday night, surpassing former President Bill Clinton’s previous mark.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: President Trump addresses joint session of Congress
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A president's speech to Congress — even without the formal gloss of a State of the Union address — is typically a time for a call to national unity and predictable claims about the country being strong. But that wasn’t President Donald Trump’s plan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Proposal would use cameras to ticket double parking in NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- A proposed pilot program aims to crack down on various curb violations, including drivers who double park in New York City. The program would use cameras to issue automatic tickets.
item.dc:creator: Kiran Dhillon, PIX 11

item.title: 46 percent say Trump changing economy for the worse: Survey
item.description: Recent polling has indicated that U.S. adults’ confidence in Trump’s ability to manage the country’s economy — the top issue in the 2024 presidential election — has been slipping.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: LA Times to give op-eds AI-generated political rating, list of alternate views
item.description: The Los Angeles Times has launched an AI-driven "bias meter" to provide readers with alternative perspectives on opinion content.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5
